### [A New Era for Cryptocurrency Regulation: The Senate's GENIUS Act](https://marchcat.com/news/a-new-era-for-cryptocurrency-regulation/)

> In a significant move, the United States Senate has advanced the first-of-its-kind legislation to regulate stablecoins, a type of cryptocurrency. The GENIUS Act, which was passed with a bipartisan vote of 66-32, aims to provide regulatory clarity and protection for consumers in the rapidly growing cryptocurrency market. This development marks a crucial step towards establishing a comprehensive framework for the regulation of stablecoins, which are digital currencies tied to the value of a specific asset, such as the US dollar.

- **Language**: en
- **Updated**: 2026-03-30

### [Crypto projects have given out 115 billion points so far](https://marchcat.com/news/crypto-projects-have-given-out-115-billion-points-so-far/)

> According to a report by The Block, crypto projects have given out 115 billion points so far. Points are off-chain numbers given to users as a “reward” for certain actions, like swapping tokens on the project’s own platform. They are typically done ahead of a potential airdrop to let users know what actions will be rewarded.

- **Language**: en
- **Updated**: 2026-03-30

### [Cryptocurrency Enters a New Phase: A Review of the Evolving Landscape](https://marchcat.com/news/cryptocurrency-enters-a-new-phase/)

> The world of cryptocurrency has undergone significant transformations since its inception and is now entering a new phase of development. This new phase is characterized by greater structure, accountability, and interaction with existing institutions, marking a significant shift from its early days as a speculative experiment. In this review, we will delve into the key aspects of this new phase, exploring the changing profile of market participants, the impact of macroeconomic conditions, and the evolving role of cryptocurrency in the global financial system.

- **Language**: en

### [Trump's National Cyber Strategy: A New Era for Crypto and Blockchain Support](https://marchcat.com/news/trump-national-cyber-strategy-a-new-era-for-crypto-and-blockchain-support/)

> The White House has recently released a new cybersecurity framework, titled President Trump's Cyber Strategy for America, which outlines the U.S. plan to strengthen its response to cyber threats. The seven-page document focuses heavily on cyber offense and deterrence, while offering relatively few details on how the policies will be implemented. However, despite its concise format, the strategy marks a significant development for the crypto industry. For the first time, cryptocurrency and blockchain technologies are explicitly mentioned as systems that must be "protected and secured."

> In a landmark decision, the Madras High Court has formally recognized cryptocurrency as a form of "property" under Indian law. This ruling, delivered by Justice N. Anand Venkatesh, has significant implications for investor rights, legal jurisdiction, and the overall crypto regulatory landscape in India. The court's declaration arose from a petition filed by an investor whose XRP holdings on the WazirX exchange were frozen following a cyberattack.
